fr.elements: Main Building; Detached Structure; Driveway; Landscaped Area; Outdoor Storage Area
fr.recommendations: Enhancing emergency responder access and operations at the property could be achieved by ensuring clear and unobstructed driveways and pathways, maintaining open sightlines especially around corners or any turns. Increasing the number of clearly marked access points could facilitate faster entry and egress for emergency vehicles. Improving visibility around key areas such as the driveway and any landscaped sections around structures may assist responders in navigating the terrain more efficiently, ensuring they can promptly access all necessary areas. Organizing the outdoor storage area so it remains easily accessible and devoid of obstacles would further support effective responder movement and staging, contributing to a more efficient emergency response operation.

wildfire.conditions: Scattered Trees; Scattered Shrubs; Limited Defensible Space; Vegetated Perimeter; Open Terrain; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Fuel Continuity; Vegetated Access Route; Tree Cluster
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space by increasing the separation between vegetation and the structure could reduce potential fire exposure. Managing vegetation concentrations, particularly adjacent to the structure and near the access route, may diminish fuel continuity and improve conditions for firefighter access. Establishing open areas around the perimeter of the structure and maintaining these areas free of dense vegetation could support suppression operations and overall preparedness. Attention to the vegetated access route is important; reducing vegetation density along this route may enhance safe passage for firefighting resources. Additionally, strategic thinning of the tree cluster and scattered trees could help preserve open terrain while maintaining long-term wildfire resilience. Focused efforts on managing scattered shrubs and maintaining clear areas around the residence could contribute to reducing pathways for fire spread.
